steps.title: Identify the transmission pattern
steps.content: The strongest clue is that the fungus spreads “from tree to nearby tree through the air” — meaning transmission depends on proximity. The trees in question are located in a “hard-to-reach valley,” emphasizing their geographic isolation.

steps.title: Connect isolation to survival
steps.content:
C explains survival through distance: if these Nacional trees were too far from the infected ones, the fungus couldn't reach them since it requires tree-to-tree proximity.

A and B address the timing of knowledge and discovery rather than the actual mechanism of survival, while D discusses chocolate value, which is unrelated to disease avoidance.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
